justification: All pass criteria met: Only v2 content used (AC1 marketing opt-out + AC3 transactional prohibition, no AC2 from v1). V2 domain rule uses absolute prohibition language ('cannot be disabled under any circumstances — this is a legal requirement'). AC3 present with exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED. Brief explicitly notes v2 supersedes v1 in task_context and contradictions_resolved section. MJ's 403 enforcement aligned with v2 prohibition throughout architecture_guidance.

observations.text: Contradictions_resolved section clearly documents that Bird v1 AC2 is invalidated by v2 and must never be implemented
Exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED appears in acceptance criteria, architecture guidance, and terminology alignment
Task_context leads with 'v2 supersedes v1' and instructs Shaq to implement against v2 + MJ only
Identifies genuine open questions (transactional_emails_enabled=true edge case, error envelope schema) that neither agent addressed
Domain rules use v2's absolute prohibition language, not v1's softer 'cannot be fully disabled'
